steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both texts discuss the Norse settlement in Greenland, but they disagree on why it ended: Text 1 blames temperature decline during the Little Ice Age, while Text 2 argues drought was the more significant cause. Despite that disagreement, both are focused on the same event: the departure of the Norse from Greenland.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The first artifacts found at a Norse settlement: neither text mentions artifacts. ✗
  • Choice B — The abandonment of the Norse settlement in Greenland: both texts center on why the Norse left Greenland — this is the shared topic regardless of which cause each text emphasizes. ✓
  • Choice C — The history of drought in Greenland: drought appears only in Text 2 as one proposed cause; it is not the shared focus of both texts. ✗
  • Choice D — The causes of the Little Ice Age: the Little Ice Age is referenced in Text 1 as a cause of the abandonment, not as the subject being researched; Text 2 doesn't focus on it at all. ✗
That matches Choice B — The abandonment of the Norse settlement in Greenland.
